The Assyrian empire, along with Egypt, had been a major force in the ancient Near East for more than two centuries before its army besieged and then destroyed Samaria. According to Sargon's record, he carried off 27,290 captives and, in accordance with Assyrian policies, relocated them to other places within his kingdom.
